title: salix discolor var. eriocephala
intro: Salix discolor var. eriocephala, also known as the River Birch or Red Birch, is a deciduous shrub or small tree that belongs to the willow family, characterized by its papery white or pink catkins and reddish-brown bark. The medicinal properties of this plant include its anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties, which can be used to treat various health issues. The health benefits of Salix discolor var. eriocephala include reducing fever, relieving digestive issues, and providing relief from respiratory problems such as bronchitis and coughs. Herbal preparations of this plant include making a tea by infusing the bark or leaves in hot water, and using the bark as a tincture or powder for medicinal purposes.
